raw_input:
[5.0/5] I like this product though This has made a visible difference in the “11” that was forming between my eyebrows. I can also tell a difference in crow’s feet. The only issue is it’s not in a functional bottle; the squirt cap doesn’t work for me with a glass bottle. So I just have to open it and use a q-tip each time to dip it out. I like this product though. [1.0/5] No. Please don’t waste your money. The bottles are impossible to open, have tried to contact the company with no result, and have no idea what is really in the bottles. [4.0/5] Digg’n it! Well... I am By all accounts a Product Junkie.. I try just about anything new or different... looking for the little black pearl... I think I found one here. I only gave 4 Stars because I’ve only had it two days (so used it 4x) so I’ll update later. I worked in the medical field for 17 years and STILL I’m one of those “Instant Gratification Hunters” knowing very well they don’t exist. “Time” is key and it’s a Fact everyone has a completely different biology , Chemistry —- etc. but I used to do Botox 2x a year. ONLY between brows (compliments of being Italian 😬) but I’m so fascinated by our chemical make-up and what we eat vs. how it affects our bodies. Anyone with common sense KNOWS there is no “miracle pill” or “miracle Serum”.. it’s s cluster of Nutrition, DNA, Genetics, (you get the gist) and is why we have such varying experiences. Doesn’t mean the products are good or bad, it just might not be the right products for some. All said I actually (so far) am loving this product. I am Confused why so many reviews complain about opening or getting the serum out of the bottle. (Tip upside down and pinch the rubber extended dropper - Viola! Perfect dropper snd cant get any more simple than that. You don’t squeeze glass. This is why the dropper is rubber do you can get (pinch) a perfect amount got your skin. Now that’s out of the way. :) Moving on.. for those who know Holistic Medicine, Essential Oils or Serums/ we know all of these keep better in glass and that’s why pretty much every serum we buy (of quality) unless it’s a cream (but creams aren’t Serums, so not sure why I went there. But anyway...) I found it quit brilliant the way they gave us 2 smaller jars opposed to 1 larger jar. Another tip- every time we open a bottle a serum etc. it deoxygenates (in English= air dilutes it’s quality and in turn that breaks down the vital ingredients. So it was well executed to have. Smaller jar. (PS: I keep My 2nd one in the fridge/ same with the 24k eye patches, but I’m going off subject again) You CAN keep your “open bottle” in the refrigerator- it’s a preference. Just don’t ever keep products in HEATED areas. So the glass is a must. And other than a “dropper” thE type of rubber tip here tells me its up to us how much to pinch off. It doesn’t need to go all over the face (does anyone get Botox injections all over🤔) so for me I pinched off (again turn upside down with a light shake while from finger tip holds the cap, soooooo easy!) but I use About the size of pea or just a bit bigger..I spot treat the obvious areas and focus most on my forhead and the remaining amount I ever don lightly, in upward strokes on my face, temple, and jaw .. if you find that amount isn’t enough for you then test a few days till you get it right and any excess (of ANY TREATMENT CREAMS OR SERUMS, always wipe the back oh your hands. “Win-Win”.... and for the opening, again I have No idea the issue (likely because I worked In the industry. Any of our injectable meds came vacuum sealed with a metal lip lock we’d call it. But there are trail tracks on the top of the metal. I use Pair of flat tweezers, find the dotted line. Insert twezers there going on the top of the lid pushing outwards (holding jar tight) until it breaks the seal. Super simple. Once broken it easily peels off. And as I mentIroned earlier, I’m. Product junkie and never ever had any work done but all for it when the time comes, but our best complexion does NOT come in s bottle but rather in food. I’m 46 and these are recent pics of my youngest daughter & I. I have 3 kids (15, 21 & 28) and I’m a grandmother :). If you want good skin, won’t bore you with my full routine. But few “Must haves” for me are Obviously a great Face wash. ALWAYS Toner, followed by an Essence. Essence is so important/ it’s like the sponge to really pull all our products in. (Exfoliate 2-3 times a week) and I lighty dry brush every morning before I wash- I could Talk for an hour on that subject! But you need dry clean skin so doing it at night. You have makeup buildup. Grime, gunk... so you don’t dry brush that. If you don’t wear any makeup.. then it’s up to you. I love My little face & neck dry brush. Up and outward. 8/10’light strokes. Then go on to your routine. Say bye bye to dead skin cells and your face received much more of the products you’re applying. So Dry brush, Wash, Tone & Essence. And then I spot apply “Botulinum”... Once dry (if in. Hurry. Use a blowdryer on cool. Dries each layer in a zap!) and I use a few more but my staples are Always the Vitamin C. There is a great inexpensive one I found. Literally changed my skin for the better. And a Good HA & Peptiide. You can find many products that have peptides in HA or HA in peptides. I prefer layering. So it’s super easy once you understand. The dropper will not pour out, nor would you want to keep opening the lid) hope this helps. and don’t forget your sunscreen ☺️ [1.0/5] No Results Unfortunately The bottle is not user friendly and the instructions, oh yeah, what instruction? If there are instructions they are written in Chinese. I don’t see one small change in my skin. It didn’t break me out nor did I get a rash or great feel. Literally I have no clue what I put on my face Morning and Night for two months. I do know my make didn’t want to stay and I use a very good foundation. [3.0/5] Don’t know how to use it. No instructions. Only some writing in Japanese. [3.0/5] Don’t waste your money I don’t see any results from this produc!
And you have to find a way to get the cream out of the bottle! Awful! Don’t waste your money,since March 2018 until today August 2..don’t see any difference! [5.0/5] no more smile lines I have spent a lot of money for products that say they plump skin, and do this or that and I was always disappointed. I gave this a try because of the ingredients, Opening this was no problem at all. I have been using it faithfully 2 x's a day. It has to be about 3 to four weeks. After the first week I saw results. I was amazed. I have really deep nasolabial folds and I spent hundreds of dollars for shots to fill them. After using this in the first week I saw a huge improvement. I have one side deeper than the other and I saw improvement in both sides but a little more on the side that was less deep. Now that side is completely filled in. The other side is catching up. I would recommend it HIGHLY. I love the fact I can save tons of money and still have the effects of the injections. This product is a God send. [5.0/5] It works It really does work!!

I thought for sure it wouldn't as it was really cheap however this is not the case.

I could see results in one week. [4.0/5] It works This product definitelly has something in it that works similar to Botox. I have had Dysport (Botox) injected in my forehead for migraines and this product gives similar effects. After applying the gel, I can’t move my forehead as easily as before. The effects are temporary and it has helped reduce the pain from the headaches. I’m currently using more of it because the effects of Dysport are wearing off (injected 2 months ago).
As far as the complaint about the bottle it comes in, you have to pry off the top (typical injection lid found on injectables), remove it and then put the replacement top on it. It wasn’t difficult. I have prescription for injectable b12 shots and every injectable I’ve seen has this same lid. They are meant for syringes with needles. I’ve also seen this in bottles for medicines for pets in which a syringe (w/o a needle) is used for the dosage.
In essence, I think it works, temporarily at least. I haven’t been using it much longer than 2 months so I can’t say what the long term effects are. However, even the injectables are predicted to last about 3 months. My last one lasted a good 1 month before the effects began to lessen. [5.0/5] PRODUCT IS Amazing lady, i have spend lotS OF money on ... PRODUCT IS Amazing lady, i have spend lotS OF money on productS. This product seem scary but it works...purchasing more. ENJOY CHICAS [5.0/5] Good Product I believe if you've maintained your face well throughout the years and you just want to continue the maintenance this product will work for you. However, if you have hardly ever or never taken care of your face and you want miracles to happen, this product will most likely not work for you. Remember living healthy plays a big role. [2.0/5] not for me i didnt notice any difference and had a few people try it too with no resuts [3.0/5] Like somebody says if you like to try different products ... Actually the bottle does open without any problem whatsoever, the only thing is there is no way to find out what ingredients. Like somebody says if you like to try different products go for it loool.... Let's see how it works and keep you post it so far I applied twice [5.0/5] this works leaves no residue ..perfect..packaging no problem at all..lovely, thank you [5.0/5] Really worked well! Erased the deep lines between my eyes and erased little wrinkles from chest [5.0/5] This stuff is AMAZING. Wow!!! This stuff is AMAZING. [5.0/5] Accurate description and price Nice product

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, customer opinions on this product are mixed, with many giving it high ratings while others were disappointed.

Positive feedback highlights that the product visibly reduces wrinkles, crow's feet, smile lines, and forehead lines, with some comparing its effects to Botox or Dysport injections. Several reviewers saw results within one to four weeks of consistent use twice daily. Customers appreciated that it could save money compared to expensive injections, and some noted it even helped reduce headache and migraine pain. A few reviewers praised the packaging and said the product left no residue.

The most common complaint was difficulty opening the bottle. Many customers struggled with the cap or dropper and felt instructions were unclear or written in a foreign language, making it hard to know how to use the product correctly. One detailed reviewer offered tips, such as turning the dropper upside down and pinching the rubber tip to dispense the serum, and suggested storing it in the refrigerator to preserve quality.

Some customers saw no results at all after months of use, and a couple reported skin irritation or rashes. A few felt it was a waste of money.

In general, those who figured out how to use the product correctly and had realistic expectations tended to be satisfied, while those who struggled with the packaging or expected quick miracles were let down.
